ELECTRICAL SAFETY AND ENERGY AUDIT REPORT FOR

INDIAN BANK – PALARIVATTOM BRANCH (2025-2026)

Disclaimer

This document has been prepared based on site observations, document reviews, and discussions conducted during the audit. The findings are limited to the conditions observed and information made available at the time of the assessment, and within the agreed scope of work.

This report is intended solely for the Client’s use to support electrical safety improvements. The

ISSUING ORGANIZATION ACCEPTS NO RESPONSIBILITY FOR ANY USE OF THIS REPORT BY THIRD PARTIES OR FOR ACTIONS TAKEN BEYOND THE INTENDED SCOPE.

All technical content remains the property of the issuing organization unless otherwise agreed in writing.

sections.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
sections.description:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

This Electrical Safety Audit Report presents a comprehensive evaluation of the electrical systems and safety measures at Indian Bank, Ernakulam zone –Palarivattom branch. The audit was conducted on 19/05/2026 in accordance with NEC-2023 guidelines, CEA Regulations 2023, Indian Bank Standards and relevant Indian Standards.

The audit team has documented all observations related to Electrical safety, which are now submitted for review to the management of M/s. Indian Bank- Ernakulam Zone, these findings are intended to guide the organization in strengthening its Electrical Safety Framework of the Branches and Offsite ATM’s.

Audit Dates: 19/05/2026

Location: Palarivattom branch

Audit Conducted By: M/s. Aura Veritas Engineering and Consultancy Pvt. Ltd.

    1. Key Findings:

The overall electrical infrastructure of the branch was found to be Operational with several areas requiring immediate attention and corrective actions to ensure compliance with electrical safety standards and to prevent potential hazards.

    1. Overall Assessment:

The electrical systems require Minor to Medium level improvements to meet NEC-2023 Indian Bank Standards compliance standards and to ensure safety of personnel, equipment, and continuity of banking operations.

This report details all observations with photographic evidence, technical measurements, and prioritized recommendations for corrective actions.

The audit encompassed a comprehensive evaluation of electrical safety practices, including:

      • Electrical Maintenance Team One to One Interview during Audit days.
      • Examination of Electrical Equipment at premises.
      • Verification of Electrical Parameters such as Load Measurements, Earthing Measurements, Lux levels measurements, Maximum Current ratings verification, Thermography measurements at each panel and Distribution boards.
      • Direct Testing of Fire and Smoke Sensors, Fire Alarm panels in the facility.
      • Validation of Electrical Equipment for critical and risky areas.
      • Assessment of Maintenance Records related to Electrical safety.
      • Inspection of all relevant Electrical Engineering and Maintenance Documents.
      • Evaluation of Compliance with Previous Audit Reports
      • Benchmarking against NEC-2023 Electrical Safety Guidelines and Branch Checklist for the Electrical Safety.

BASIC TERMINOLOGY

Key Electrical Engineering Terms Everyone Should Know

Understanding these terms is crucial for anyone working with electrical systems, whether you're conducting audits, managing installations, or ensuring safety compliance.

Basic Electrical Quantities

    • Voltage (V): The electrical potential difference between two points. Measured in volts.
    • Current (I): The flow of electric charge. Measured in amperes (A).
    • Resistance (R): Opposition to current flow. Measured in ohms (Ω).
    • Power (P): The rate at which electrical energy is consumed or generated. Measured in watts (W).
    • Energy (E): Total power consumed over time. Measured in kilowatt-hours (kWh).

Components & Devices

    • Circuit Breaker: A safety device that interrupts current flow in case of overload or fault.
    • Transformer: A device that changes voltage levels between circuits.
    • Capacitor: Stores electrical energy temporarily and releases it when needed.
    • Inductor: Stores energy in a magnetic field when current flows through it.
    • Relay: Electrically operated switch used for control and automation.

System Concepts

    • Single-phase vs. Three-phase: Types of AC power systems; three-phase is more efficient for industrial use.
    • Earthing (Grounding): Connecting electrical systems to the earth to prevent shock hazards.
    • Load: The amount of electrical power consumed by devices or systems.
    • Short Circuit: An abnormal connection causing excessive current flow, often leading to damage or fire.
    • Overload: When a circuit carries more current than it's designed for,

Measurement & Safety

    • Insulation Resistance: Measures the effectiveness of insulation in preventing current leakage.
    • Thermography: Infrared imaging used to detect hot spots in electrical systems.
    • Harmonics: Distortions in electrical waveforms that can affect equipment performance.
    • RCD (Residual Current Device): Detects leakage currents and disconnects power to prevent electrocution.
    • Lux Level: Measurement of light intensity, relevant in lighting audits.

Codes & Standards

    • IS Codes: Indian Standards for fire safety and protection.
    • NEC-2024 Guidelines
    • BS EN: British/European standards for fire equipment and systems.

TYPES OF ELECTRICAL INJURIES

INTRODUCTION

Indian Bank is a trusted banking leader with strong national and regional presence, offering comprehensive financial services and robust ATM coverage for convenient banking. Branches and ATMs in Ernakulam serve local customers with wide-ranging solutions and digital support.

    1. AIMS AND OBJECTIVES OF ELECTRICAL SAFETY & ENERGY AUDIT
      • Verify Electrical Safety of the Indian Bank branches developed/implemented in accordance with system safety program plan requirement as per NEC-2023 Guidelines and NBC-2016 Guidelines.
      • Electrical Audit includes testing and verification of input from Mains, Input and Output of UPS, Condition of UPS, AC wiring and functioning of AC units, all wires, fuse carriers, MCBs Earth lighting and other circuits, Generator etc. Tests as per the format area are to be completed for each branch.
      • Assess effectiveness of the agency’s system Electrical safety systems.
      • Identify potential Safety hazards in the operational system and weaknesses in the system safety programs.
      • Verify prior corrective actions are being tracked for closure.
      • Recommend improvements to the system safety program.
      • Provide management with assessment of status and adequacy of system safety program.
      • Assure continuing evaluation of safety related issues, awareness and reporting.

SCOPE FOR ELECTRICAL AUDIT AND ENERGY AUDIT

Electrical Safety Audit Scope in Compliance with NEC-2023 & IS Standards

Standards & Compliance

    • Conduct audit in accordance with NEC-2023, NBC-2016 relevant Indian Standards
    • Ensure alignment with statutory and regulatory electrical safety norms and BEE Norms.

Preventive Maintenance Review

    • Evaluate the Electrical Preventive Maintenance (EPM) system
    • Check for schedule adherence, documentation, and effectiveness.

Incident & Accident Review

    • Analyse past Electrical accidents and incidents within the facility.
    • Identify root causes and assess corrective/preventive measures taken.

Procedure & Documentation Review

    • Review Electrical Safety System procedures and Energy Management Procedures for adequacy and implementation.
    • Examine key documents and Conduct Electrical Safety Tests:
      • Register of Electrical Installations
      • Test Conducted:
        • Insulation Resistance Test
        • Earth Resistance Test
        • LT Panel Equipment Tests
      • Data Sheets of critical electrical installations

Thermography Analysis

    • Review and Conduct Thermography test results of all electrical installations.
    • Identify hotspots, loose connections, and thermal anomalies.

Emergency Power Systems

    • Assess supply reliability and readiness of emergency systems:
  • Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS)
  • Battery Backup Systems

Report & Recommendations

    • Compile findings into a comprehensive audit report.
    • Provide actionable recommendations for safety and operational improvements.

METHODOLOGY

The Electrical Safety and Energy Audit include the review of documentation with regard to the scope covered in audit, an on-site visit, including Electrical Safety Measures, Energy Saving measures, and data collection, their review and analysis. Following methodology in detail:

EARTHING RESISTANCE:

Earthing resistance is the resistance between the grounding electrode and the surrounding soil. It determines how effectively fault currents, lightning surges, or leakage currents are dissipated into the earth.

Important for all Applications:

Safety: Prevents electric shock hazards by directing fault currents safely into the ground.

Equipment Protection: Reduces the risk of damage to electrical systems during faults or lightning strikes.

Voltage Stabilization: Helps maintain consistent voltage levels across the system.

Ideal Resistance Values:

sections.title: LOAD MEASUREMENT STUDY
sections.description:
LOAD MEASUREMENT STUDY

Load Flow Analysis is a computational method used to determine the steady-state operating conditions of an electrical power system. It evaluates how electrical power—both real (P) and reactive (Q)—flows through the network under specific load conditions.

Important for all Applications:

  • Required by NEC 220.87 for determining existing loads before adding new equipment
  • Prevents overloads, phase imbalance, and fire hazards
  • Ensures panel boards and feeders operate within safe limits
  • Identifies peak demand periods and power wastage
  • Supports Power factor correction and efficiency improvements
  • Detects the Irregular load distribution, harmonics and voltage drop
  • Avoid Cost retrofits

sections.title: THERMOGRAPHY
sections.description:
THERMOGRAPHY

Thermal imaging is a sophisticated and non-invasive technique that utilizes infrared technology to detect heat emissions from various objects. It converts infrared energy into a visible light display, helping detect abnormal heat patterns caused by overloads or impending equipment failures.

Important for all Applications:

  • Preventing arc flash, insulation failure, and fire hazard
  • Detecting hot spots in panels, breakers, and busbars
  • Identifying loose connections and phase imbalance
  • Monitoring transformer heating and motor bearings

sections.title: TOTAL LIGHTING CONNECTED LOAD
sections.description:
TOTAL LIGHTING CONNECTED LOAD

Lighting connected loads refer to the total electrical load attributed to lighting fixtures and systems within a facility.

Checklist during our Inspection:

  • Verify fixture type and wattage
  • Cross-check with lighting layout drawings
  • Measure actual load using clamp meter
  • Compare with design load and NEC/IS standards
  • Identify retrofit opportunities for energy saving

Lighting Load for M/s. Indian Bank – Ernakulam zone - Palarivattom branch:

Observation:

During the Site Audit and Recording the Actual Loads at all Rooms, we are Satisfactory with the Results obtained from the Clamp meter and Engineering Predictions. We also found that majority of the Lighting Loads found Acceptable.

Opportunity for Improvement:

Based on the Site Audit findings and anomalies found during the normal operation please follow the below basic steps to increase the Efficiency of the Equipment and Proper Load distribution across all Common rooms.

  • Old lights are to be are to be replaced with Energy efficient Lights to minimize the Load during Peak hours.

sections.title: CONCLUSION
sections.description:
CONCLUSION

Based on the Site findings, technical document verification, Validation of test reports, Our Site Testing (Earth Resistance, Thermography, Load Imbalance Study assessment, Lightning Load Assessment, Lux Study Assessment) we found the below mentioned recommendations are to be taken care in order to maintain the NEC-2023 Guidelines, BEE Standards and Electrical Equipment & Personnel Safety. Final conclusions/recommendations are listed in Appendix 1.

tables.title: Data Grid
columns: Detailed Scope of Work for Rectifying the Observations/Shortcomings
rows: Common Neutral Line Cable Temperature is High is due to Harmonic currents raised due to Variable loads such as Computers, Air Conditioners etc, In order to avoid this scenario we recommend to reduce the R-Phase load and Re-distribute to other Phases, Especially Group all Non-Linear Loads and supply them via separate Busbars, In case the Problem persists after lining all Non-Linear loads, change the Neutral Cable Size to the Next Cross sectional size to handle the Additional Non-Linear Current and Heat dissipation.

It is recommended to conduct a thorough inspection of earth electrodes and connections to check for rust, corrosion, or physical damage. Engage a certified third-party consultant to measure the earth resistance using a calibrated earth tester, ensuring that all readings comply with standard safety requirements.
UPS connection split up loads to be properly maintained as per emergency load distribution matrix

Heavy duty contact points to be checked for the firm connection to avoid overheating and minimize power loss

fields.title: Whether exhaust fans for ventilation of panel room / electrical room / UPS room / DG set room is provided and no paper, old material or any other scrap is kept near DB/ panels / ups / batteries etc.

Whether isolating switches are provided for switching off of the non essential loads in premises during night and main switch to switch off the power supply to the building / floor in case of fire / emergency.

Whether proper preventive maintenance after opening of the electrical boards and distribution boards are carried out by the license holder electrician or skilled technicians of equipment manufacturer / service providers.

Whether appropriate timers used in the changeover of air conditioners for server room ACs and for signage boards to make auto on / off (for schedule timings). the thermostat of the ACs at server room should be set to say 26°c, so that it will run only when the temperature is too high (to minimize chances of fire due to idle running of the ACs during night)
